trigger.reason: Google DeepMind released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with expanded Gemini AI features integrated into core OS — a major product release from a top-tier lab that will draw broad market attention and trigger sympathy-fade selling in adjacent software/SaaS stocks.

primary_event.headline: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
primary_event.summary: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades, accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google's latest AI models to its devices. This represents a major OS-level integration of Gemini across Google's consumer hardware ecosystem.

secondary_events.headline: ChatGPT's market share slips below 50% for first time

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Google DeepMind's release of Android 17 / Wear OS 7 with expanded Gemini AI integration across Google's consumer hardware ecosystem.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a consumer OS / mobile Gemini integration — its plausible blast radius is mobile app developers, app-store monetization, and consumer-facing SaaS productivity, NOT semiconductor picks-and-shovels or vertical enterprise workflow software. Today's sympathy tape pulled in a cluster of semi-cap names (PLAB, ONTO, AEIS, AMBA) and embedded vertical SaaS (PAYC, MANH, HCAT) whose filings show essentially no exposure to a consumer OS release. Best false-victim candidates are the semi picks-and-shovels (whose business is structurally LONG AI capex) and the deeply-embedded vertical SaaS plays where switching costs and integration depth are the real moat.

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K Risk Factors discuss dependence on the microelectronics industry, photomask demand tied to chip design activity, and customer concentration — no AI-as-competitive-threat language. Photomasks are a required input for every semiconductor including AI accelerators.
discoveries.thesis: A consumer OS release adding Gemini features to Android phones has zero mechanical connection to photomask demand. If anything, more AI compute = more advanced-node chip designs = more photomask demand. The selloff is pure thematic semi-sympathy tape with the sign flipped — Photronics is a beneficiary of AI capex, not a victim of a consumer mobile feature release.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued AI-accelerator design activity at TSMC/Samsung/Intel showing up in PLAB's next quarterly design release commentary; stable two-largest-customer concentration metric.
discoveries.what_kills: An actual cyclical downturn in semi capex unrelated to today's trigger; loss of a top-two customer; further Section 232 / China export restrictions that materially reduce PLAB's addressable Asia revenue (82% of total).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semi picks-and-shovels routinely trade off on AI-lab consumer-product news despite being structurally long the same trend — the further you are from the application layer, the more mechanical the sympathy fade.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PLAB was caught in a broad tech/semi sympathy tape on the Google announcement despite zero business overlap with mobile OS features.
research_pointers: Check PLAB's most recent design-release trend commentary for AI-driven advanced-node activity
Confirm no 8-K customer-loss filing concurrent with today's move
Compare PLAB's drop to broader SOX/SMH move — is this pure beta or worse?

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K Risk Factors mention 'The use or anticipated use of new and evolving technologies, such as AI, by us or third parties may increase or create new operational risks' as a general operational risk — not as a threat to ONTO's core process-control metrology business. Core business is inspection/metrology for advanced semiconductor nodes.
discoveries.thesis: Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity — more compute = smaller geometries = more inspection passes per wafer. A Gemini-on-Android release has no mechanical link to wafer fab inspection demand. The sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued strength in advanced-node spending from TSMC/Samsung/Intel; Onto-Rigaku deal closing on terms; advanced-packaging inspection demand commentary in next earnings.
discoveries.what_kills: Memory market downturn affecting Onto's specialty-semi exposure; delay or unfavorable terms on the Rigaku transaction; broader semi-cap capex air pocket.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: ONTO sold off with semi-cap basket on broad tech weakness following the Google announcement despite no exposure to consumer OS or mobile app economics.
research_pointers: Track Rigaku acquisition closing progress — referenced in 10-Q risk factor update
Check semi-cap peer-group move (LRCX, AMAT, KLAC) — is ONTO trading in line or worse?
Look at the five-day prior-run-up — some of today's drop may simply be profit-taking on an extended week

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly states: 'infrastructure investments in artificial intelligence (AI) have increased substantially, which is driving significant demand increases in the Data Center Computing market. We accelerated investments to increase capacity...' AI is a stated revenue tailwind, not a competitive threat.
discoveries.thesis: AEIS's own 10-K identifies AI infrastructure as a material growth driver. Power conversion for fabs and AI data centers is structurally long the buildout that Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude inference all require. A consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery demand. The sympathy drop on weak volume with a still-positive five-day looks like a clean overshoot.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued data-center computing segment growth in next earnings; further AI-capex commentary from hyperscaler customers.
discoveries.what_kills: Hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction; loss of design-win share to a competitor; Data Center Computing margins continuing to compress as that segment grows.
discoveries.what_to_learn: When a company's own Risk Factors explicitly call out AI infrastructure as a demand tailwind, an AI-news sympathy drop is mechanically backwards and the highest-conviction false-victim setup.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AEIS was swept up in the broader semi/industrial tech sympathy fade on the Gemini announcement despite being a stated AI-capex beneficiary.
research_pointers: Re-read most recent Data Center Computing segment revenue growth — AI exposure is explicit
Check whether peers (MKSI, ENTG) moved similarly — confirms basket sympathy vs name-specific
Five-day move was positive going into today — some of the drop is profit-taking, not pure sympathy

discoveries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K acknowledges 'future disruptive technologies, such as AI and machine learning' as a competitive risk and notes potential headcount-reduction impact from AI (since PAYC charges per-employee). However, the core business is regulated payroll tax compliance, jurisdictional law, ACH/banking integration, and audit-trail of-record — not a text interface that Gemini can replicate.
discoveries.thesis: A consumer mobile OS adding Gemini features doesn't touch payroll-tax filing, ACH funds movement, multi-jurisdictional employment law, or PAYC's bank-of-record relationship with employee comp. The moat is regulatory complexity and integration depth, not the user-facing UI. PAYC was caught in broad SaaS sympathy tape; mobile-OS AI integration is not the relevant threat vector.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable client retention metrics in next 10-Q; no acceleration in per-employee headcount declines at clients; continued sales-cycle stability.
discoveries.what_kills: Acceleration in clients reducing headcount due to AI (PAYC's own stated risk — they bill per employee); a vertical AI-payroll competitor showing real traction; PAYC client churn data turning negative.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical workflow SaaS with regulatory moats (payroll, healthcare compliance, supply chain WMS) routinely sells off on every consumer AI release — the real exposure is downstream (client headcount), not direct substitution.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PAYC moved with broad application-software sympathy fade despite the trigger being a consumer mobile OS release with no overlap to enterprise HCM/payroll workflows.
research_pointers: Track PAYC's annual revenue retention rate trend
Monitor per-employee billing trends — early warning signal for AI-driven headcount erosion
Five-day is already negative — confirm this isn't part of a name-specific guidance issue

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K discusses AI/ML as a growth opportunity ('a growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services') rather than primarily as a competitive threat. The main threat language relates to AI/ML markets not developing as anticipated, not to DOCN being displaced.
discoveries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to a consumer Android+Gemini release. Their AI inference offerings (GPU droplets, GenAI platform) actually align them with the trend. The mechanical link from 'Google ships Gemini in Android' to 'DigitalOcean's SMB cloud subscriptions decline' is nonexistent.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued AI/ML revenue growth disclosed in next 10-Q; stable developer-customer expansion rate; no decline in DNE Customer count.
discoveries.what_kills: Hyperscaler price-cuts on small instances making DOCN's pricing edge less meaningful; AI/ML revenue traction failing to materialize in next quarter; SMB churn acceleration.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN caught in cloud/software sympathy fade despite the trigger event having no mechanical relevance to SMB cloud infrastructure demand.
research_pointers: Read most recent AI/ML revenue disclosure — DOCN is positioning as AI beneficiary
Check DNE Customer growth trend
Compare DOCN move to other small-cap cloud peers — is this basket-wide or DOCN-specific?

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K discusses generative/agentic AI extensively but frames it as both a feature MANH is adding to its own products AND as an operational risk — not as an existential threat to its WMS platform. Core customer base is large retailers with multi-year WMS deployments integrated with ERP and physical warehouse hardware.
discoveries.thesis: A consumer mobile OS Gemini integration has no plausible mechanical link to enterprise warehouse-management system demand. WMS is deeply embedded with physical material handling, ERP, and supply-chain processes — switching cost, not the AI text layer, is the moat. The five-day was already negative going into today; the broader application-software sympathy fade caught MANH in the basket.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable large-customer cloud-subscription bookings; continued WMS deal flow; no acceleration in customer evaluation of AI-native point solutions.
discoveries.what_kills: A real AI-native WMS or OMS competitor demonstrating large-customer wins; retail vertical capex pullback (MANH's largest end market); guidance cut tied to deal-cycle elongation.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Deeply-embedded vertical SaaS with multi-year deployments and physical-system integration is the canonical false-victim profile on consumer AI news days.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: MANH was caught in broad SaaS sympathy fade on the Gemini consumer announcement despite no overlap with mobile OS or app-store dynamics.
research_pointers: Five-day is already negative — confirm no concurrent guidance or analyst-downgrade catalyst
Check whether large-deal commentary in last earnings remained stable
Compare to retail-vertical SaaS peers — basket move or name-specific?

discoveries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K cites Epic, Oracle Health, Optum, and IBM as competitors and acknowledges EHR companies are increasingly entering data analytics — a real but pre-existing competitive risk, not specifically tied to consumer AI. No language identifying general-purpose AI agents as displacing the data-warehouse + analytics moat.
discoveries.thesis: HCAT has plenty of real issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, end-market funding cuts) but a consumer Android Gemini release is not one of them. HIPAA, BAA requirements, and EHR integration depth prevent general-purpose AI agents from touching provider data. The drop is sympathy-fade on top of an already-pressured name. Confidence intentionally limited because HCAT has genuine, name-specific issues that make this less clean.
discoveries.what_confirms: Project Nexus delivering targeted cost savings; Ignite migration stabilizing client base; no acceleration in DOS churn.
discoveries.what_kills: Further Medicaid funding cuts pressuring health-system IT spend; client losses to Epic/Oracle accelerating; Ignite migration causing more revenue churn than guided.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sometimes a name has BOTH real problems AND an unjustified-on-this-particular-trigger sympathy drop. Calibrate confidence down rather than skip — but flag the noise.
discoveries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: HCAT caught in healthcare-IT/SaaS sympathy fade despite the trigger having no relevance to provider analytics platforms.
research_pointers: Confirm no fresh restructuring or guidance-cut 8-K today
Read Project Nexus details and timing carefully — already-troubled name
This is a sub-$2 stock with $130M market cap — liquidity/microcap risk is material

skipped.reason: Curation calls Ambarella a 'direct AI beneficiary' (edge inference SoCs), which the filings confirm — making this a real beneficiary, not a false-victim setup needing OVERDONE flag; move is modest and confidence in a clean call is limited by AMBA's history of independent customer-concentration volatility (WT at ~70% of revenue).
Move is positive (+3.17%), so no sympathy fade to fade; doesn't fit the OVERDONE-on-negative-move screen criteria.
Real-victim control with intentional plausible exposure to AI voice agents, AND filings are entirely unavailable — cannot make a defensible threat assessment either way.

_trigger.headline: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
_trigger.summary: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7 with new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades, accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google's latest AI models to its devices. This represents a major OS-level integration of Gemini across Google's consumer hardware ecosystem.
